info.title: Exa Search API
info.description: A comprehensive API for internet-scale search, allowing users to perform queries and retrieve results from a wide variety of sources using embeddings-based and traditional search.

operations.description: Perform a search with a Exa prompt-engineered query and retrieve a list of relevant results. Optionally get contents.
Find similar links to the link provided. Optionally get contents.

operations.summary: Generate an answer from search results
operations.description: Performs a search based on the query and generates either a direct answer or a detailed summary with citations, depending on the query type.

query.description: The question or query to answer.

stream.description: If true, the response is returned as a server-sent events (SSS) stream.

text.description: If true, the response includes full text content in the search results

outputSchema.description: A [JSON Schema Draft 7](https://json-schema.org/draft-07) specification for the desired answer structure. When provided, the answer will be returned as a structured object matching the schema instead of a plain string.

properties.description: An object where each key is a property name and each value is a JSON Schema describing that property (with `type`, `description`, etc).

additionalProperties.description: Whether to allow properties not listed in `properties`.

operations.summary: Create a research task with instructions and an output schema

instructions.description: Instructions for what the research task should accomplish

schema.description: A JsonSchema specification of the desired output. See https://json-schema.org/draft-07.

inferSchema.description: When set to true and no output schema is provided, an LLM will generate an output schema.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to paginate through the results

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of results to return

operations.summary: Get a research task by id

id.description: The temporary ID for the document.

url.description: The URL of the search result.

title.description: The title of the search result.

author.description: If available, the author of the content.

publishedDate.description: An estimate of the creation date, from parsing HTML content. Format is YYYY-MM-DD.

text.description: The full text content of each source. Only present when includeText is enabled.

image.description: The URL of the image associated with the search result, if available.

favicon.description: The URL of the favicon for the search result's domain, if available.

answer.description: The generated answer based on search results. Returns a string by default, or a structured object matching the provided outputSchema.

citations.description: Search results used to generate the answer.

oneOf.description: If true, returns full page text with default settings. If false, disables text return.
Advanced options for controlling text extraction. Use this when you need to limit text length or include HTML structure.

maxCharacters.description: Maximum character limit for the full page text. Useful for controlling response size and API costs.

includeHtmlTags.description: Include HTML tags in the response, which can help LLMs understand text structure and formatting.

verbosity.description:
Controls the verbosity level of returned content. Requires livecrawl: "always" to take effect.
- compact: Most concise output, main content only (default)
- standard: Balanced content with more detail
- full: Complete content including all sections

includeSections.description: Only include content from these semantic page sections. Requires livecrawl: "always" to take effect.

excludeSections.description: Exclude content from these semantic page sections. Requires livecrawl: "always" to take effect.

highlights.description: Text snippets the LLM identifies as most relevant from each page.

oneOf.description: If true, returns highlights with default settings. If false, disables highlights.
Advanced options for controlling highlight extraction. Use this when you need to customize the number of sentences, highlights per URL, or provide a custom query.

maxCharacters.description: Maximum number of characters to return for highlights. Controls the total length of highlight text returned per URL.

numSentences.description: Deprecated: use maxCharacters instead. The number of sentences to return for each snippet.

highlightsPerUrl.description: Deprecated: use maxCharacters instead. The number of snippets to return for each result.

query.description: Custom query to direct the LLM's selection of highlights.
Custom query for the LLM-generated summary.

schema.description:
JSON schema for structured output from summary. 
See https://json-schema.org/overview/what-is-jsonschema for JSON Schema documentation.

livecrawl.description:
**Deprecated**: Use `maxAgeHours` instead for more precise control over content freshness.

Options for livecrawling pages.
'never': Disable livecrawling (default for neural search).
'fallback': Livecrawl when cache is empty.
'preferred': Always try to livecrawl, but fall back to cache if crawling fails.
'always': Always live-crawl, never use cache. Only use if you cannot tolerate any cached content. This option is not recommended unless consulted with the Exa team.

livecrawlTimeout.description: The timeout for livecrawling in milliseconds.

maxAgeHours.description:
Maximum age of cached content in hours. Controls when livecrawling is triggered based on content freshness.
- Positive value (e.g. 24): Use cached content if it's less than this many hours old, otherwise livecrawl.
- 0: Always livecrawl, never use cache.
- -1: Never livecrawl, always use cache.
- Omit (default): Livecrawl as fallback only when no cached content exists.

subpages.description: The number of subpages to crawl. The actual number crawled may be limited by system constraints.

subpageTarget.description: Keyword to find specific subpages of search results. Can be a single string or an array of strings, comma delimited.

links.description: Number of URLs to return from each webpage.

imageLinks.description: Number of images to return for each result.

oneOf.description: Deprecated: Use highlights or text instead. Returns page contents as a combined context string.
Deprecated: Use highlights or text instead. Returns page contents as a combined context string.

maxCharacters.description: Deprecated. Maximum character limit for the context string.

numResults.description: Number of results to return (up to thousands of results available for custom plans)

includeDomains.description: List of domains to include in the search. If specified, results will only come from these domains.

excludeDomains.description: List of domains to exclude from search results. If specified, no results will be returned from these domains.

startCrawlDate.description: Crawl date refers to the date that Exa discovered a link. Results will include links that were crawled after this date. Must be specified in ISO 8601 format.

endCrawlDate.description: Crawl date refers to the date that Exa discovered a link. Results will include links that were crawled before this date. Must be specified in ISO 8601 format.

startPublishedDate.description: Only links with a published date after this will be returned. Must be specified in ISO 8601 format.

endPublishedDate.description: Only links with a published date before this will be returned. Must be specified in ISO 8601 format.

includeText.description: List of strings that must be present in webpage text of results. Currently, only 1 string is supported, of up to 5 words.

excludeText.description: List of strings that must not be present in webpage text of results. Currently, only 1 string is supported, of up to 5 words. Checks from the first 1000 words of the webpage text.

oneOf.description: Deprecated: Use highlights or text instead. Returns page contents as a combined context string.
Deprecated: Use highlights or text instead. Returns page contents as a combined context string.

maxCharacters.description: Deprecated. Maximum character limit for the context string.

moderation.description: Enable content moderation to filter unsafe content from search results.

name.description: Name of the funding round (e.g., "Series A", "Seed").

EntityCompanyProperties.description: Structured properties for a company entity.

EntityDateRange.description: Date range for work history entries.

EntityPersonPropertiesCompanyRef.description: Reference to a company in work history.

id.description: Exa entity ID for the company.

EntityPersonPropertiesWorkHistoryEntry.description: A single work history entry for a person.

EntityPersonProperties.description: Structured properties for a person entity.

CompanyEntity.description: Structured entity data for a company.

id.description: Exa entity ID.

PersonEntity.description: Structured entity data for a person.

id.description: Exa entity ID.

Entity.description: Structured entity data for company or person search results. Only returned for category=company or category=people searches.

title.description: The title of the search result.

url.description: The URL of the search result.

publishedDate.description: An estimate of the creation date, from parsing HTML content. Format is YYYY-MM-DD.

author.description: If available, the author of the content.

score.description: A number from 0 to 1 representing similarity between the query/url and the result.

id.description: The temporary ID for the document. Useful for /contents endpoint.

image.description: The URL of an image associated with the search result, if available.

favicon.description: The URL of the favicon for the search result's domain.

total.description: Total dollar cost for your request

breakDown.description: Breakdown of costs by operation type

neuralSearch.description: Cost of your neural search operations

deepSearch.description: Cost of your deep search operations

contentText.description: Cost of your text content retrieval

perRequestPrices.description: Standard price per request for different operations

neuralSearch_1_25_results.description: Standard price for neural search with 1-25 results

neuralSearch_26_100_results.description: Standard price for neural search with 26-100 results

neuralSearch_100_plus_results.description: Standard price for neural search with 100+ results

deepSearch_1_25_results.description: Standard price for deep search with 1-25 results

deepSearch_26_100_results.description: Standard price for deep search with 26-100 results

perPagePrices.description: Standard price per page for different content operations

contentText.description: Standard price per page for text content

contentHighlight.description: Standard price per page for highlights

contentSummary.description: Standard price per page for summaries

instructions.description: The instructions or query for the research task

data.description: The research results data conforming to the specified schema

operations.description:
Creates a new Webset with optional search, import, and enrichment configurations. The Webset will automatically begin processing once created.

You can specify an `externalId` to reference the Webset with your own identifiers for easier integration.

Returns a list of Websets.

You can paginate through the results using the `cursor` parameter.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to paginate through the results

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of Websets to return

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id or externalId of the Webset.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Expand the response with the specified resources

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id or externalId of the Webset

operations.description:
Deletes a Webset.

Once deleted, the Webset and all its Items will no longer be available.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id or externalId of the Webset

operations.description:
Cancels all operations being performed on a Webset.

Any enrichment or search will be stopped and the Webset will be marked as `idle`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id or externalId of the Webset

operations.description:
Preview how a search query will be decomposed before creating a webset. This endpoint performs the same query analysis that happens during webset creation, allowing you to see the detected entity type, generated search criteria, and available enrichment columns in advance.

Use this to help users understand how their search will be interpreted before committing to a full webset creation.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Weather you want to search for a preview list of items or not

operations.description:
Webhooks let you get notifications when things happen in your Websets. When you create a webhook, you choose which events you want to know about and where to send the notifications.

When an event happens, Exa sends an HTTP POST request to your webhook URL with:
- Event details (type, time, ID)
- Full data of what triggered the event
- A signature to verify the request came from Exa

The webhook starts as `active` and begins getting notifications right away. You'll get a secret key for checking webhook signatures - save this safely as it's only shown once when you create the webhook.

Get a list of all webhooks in your account.
The results come in pages. Use `limit` to set how many webhooks to get per page (up to 200). Use `cursor` to get the next page of results.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to paginate through the results

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of results to return

operations.description:
Get information about a webhook using its ID.
The webhook secret is not shown here for security - you only get it when you first create the webhook.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the webhook

operations.description:
Change a webhook's settings. You can update:
- Events: Add or remove which events you want to hear about - URL: Change where notifications are sent - Metadata: Update custom data linked to the webhook

Changes happen right away. If you change the events list, the webhook will start or stop getting notifications for those events immediately.

The webhook keeps its current status (`active` or `inactive`) when you update it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the webhook

operations.description:
Remove a webhook from your account. Once deleted, the webhook stops getting notifications right away and cannot be brought back.

Important notes: - The webhook stops working as soon as you delete it - You cannot undo this - you'll need to create a new webhook if you want it back - Any notifications currently being sent may still complete

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the webhook

parameters.name: createdBefore
parameters.description: Filter events created before or at this timestamp (inclusive). Must be a valid ISO 8601 datetime string. All times are in UTC.

parameters.name: createdAfter
parameters.description: Filter events created after or at this timestamp (inclusive). Must be a valid ISO 8601 datetime string. All times are in UTC.

Get a single Event by id.

You can subscribe to Events by creating a Webhook.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the event

operations.description:
Creates a new `Monitor` to continuously keep your Websets updated with fresh data.

Monitors automatically run on your defined schedule to ensure your Websets stay current without manual intervention:

- **Find new content**: Execute `search` operations to discover fresh items matching your criteria
- **Update existing content**: Run `refresh` operations to update items contents and enrichments
- **Automated scheduling**: Configure `cron` expressions and `timezone` for precise scheduling control
Lists all monitors for the Webset.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to paginate through the results

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of results to return

parameters.name: websetId
parameters.description: The id of the Webset to list monitors for

parameters.description: The id of the Monitor to get the run for

operations.description:
Creates a new import to upload your data into Websets. Imports can be used to:

- **Enrich**: Enhance your data with additional information using our AI-powered enrichment engine
- **Search**: Query your data using Websets' agentic search with natural language filters
- **Exclude**: Prevent duplicate or already known results from appearing in your searches

Once the import is created, you can upload your data to the returned `uploadUrl` until `uploadValidUntil` (by default 1 hour).
Lists all imports for the Webset.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to paginate through the results

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of results to return

operations.description: Gets a specific import.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the Import

operations.description: Updates a import configuration.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the Import

operations.description: Deletes a import.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the Import

description.description: Provide a description of the enrichment task you want to perform to each Webset Item.

format.description:
Format of the enrichment response.

We automatically select the best format based on the description. If you want to explicitly specify the format, you can do so here.

options.description: When the format is options, the different options for the enrichment agent to choose from.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs you want to associate with this object.

search.description: Create initial search for the Webset.

query.description:
Natural language search query describing what you are looking for.

Be specific and descriptive about your requirements, characteristics, and any constraints that help narrow down the results.

Any URLs provided will be crawled and used as additional context for the search.

count.description:
Number of Items the Webset will attempt to find.

The actual number of Items found may be less than this number depending on the search complexity.

criteria.description:
Criteria every item is evaluated against.

It's not required to provide your own criteria, we automatically detect the criteria from all the information provided in the query. Only use this when you need more fine control.

recall.description:
Whether to provide an estimate of how many total relevant results could exist for this search.
Result of the analysis will be available in the `recall` field within the search request.

exclude.description: Sources (existing imports or websets) to exclude from search results. Any results found within these sources will be omitted to prevent finding them during search.

id.description: The ID of the source to exclude.

scope.description: Limit this search to only consider candidates from the listed sources (existing Imports or Websets). Scope applies per-search; if you run another search and want to stay within the same dataset, pass scope again. When scope is present, the search behavior is OVERRIDE (replaces results rather than appending). Note: Using the same Import in both top-level import and search.scope will return a 400 error.

id.description: The ID of the source to search.

definition.description: What the relationship of the entities you hope to find is relative to the entities contained in the provided source. Only needed for hop searches (graph traversal) from the source entities to related targets. Examples: "investors of", "current employer", "employees at". Omit for simple filtering within the source.

limit.description: Number of related entities to find per source entity (fanout). Only used for hop searches. Range: 1-10.

import.description: Attach/load data from existing Imports or Websets into this Webset. For CSV Imports, this schedules ingestion and creates a staging pool of items (ImportItems do not automatically appear as Webset Items; searches create Webset Items). This does not filter searches. To filter a search to only look within an Import or Webset, use search.scope instead.

id.description: The ID of the source to search.

enrichments.description:
Add enrichments to extract additional data from found items.

Enrichments automatically search for and extract specific information (like contact details, funding data, employee counts, etc.) from each item added to your Webset.

exclude.description: Global exclusion sources (existing imports or websets) that apply to all operations within this Webset. Any results found within these sources will be omitted across all search and import operations.

id.description: The ID of the source to exclude.

externalId.description:
The external identifier for the webset.

You can use this to reference the Webset by your own internal identifiers.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs you want to associate with this object.

id.description: The unique identifier for the search

websetId.description: The unique identifier for the Webset this search belongs to

query.description: The query used to create the search.

entity.description:
The entity the search will return results for.

When no entity is provided during creation, we will automatically select the best entity based on the query.

criteria.description: The criteria the search will use to evaluate the results. If not provided, we will automatically generate them for you.

successRate.description: Value between 0 and 100 representing the percentage of results that meet the criterion.

count.description: The number of results the search will attempt to find. The actual number of results may be less than this number depending on the search complexity.

exclude.description: Sources (existing imports or websets) used to omit certain results to be found during the search.

scope.description:
The scope of the search. By default, there is no scope - thus searching the web.

If provided during creation, the search will only be performed on the sources provided.

definition.description: What the relationship of the entities you hope to find is relative to the entities contained in the provided source.

found.description: The number of results found so far

analyzed.description: The number of results analyzed so far

completion.description: The completion percentage of the search

timeLeft.description: The estimated time remaining in seconds, null if unknown

recall.description: Recall metrics for the search, null if not yet computed or requested.

total.description: The estimated total number of potential matches

min.description: The minimum estimated total number of potential matches

max.description: The maximum estimated total number of potential matches

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs you want to associate with this object.

canceledAt.description: The date and time the search was canceled

canceledReason.description: The reason the search was canceled

createdAt.description: The date and time the search was created

updatedAt.description: The date and time the search was updated

entity.description: The type of entity the import contains.

updatedAt.description: When the import was last updated

This will be automatically generated based on the description and format.

options.description: When the format is options, the different options for the enrichment agent to choose from.

This will be automatically generated based on the description and format.

config.description:
Specify the search parameters for the Monitor.

By default, the search parameters (query, entity and criteria) from the last search are used when no parameters are provided.

query.description: The query to search for. By default, the query from the last search is used.

criteria.description: The criteria to search for. By default, the criteria from the last search is used.

count.description: The maximum number of results to find

behavior.description: The behaviour of the Search when it is added to a Webset.

lastRun.description: The last run of the monitor

nextRunAt.description: Date and time when the next run will occur in

Natural language search query describing what you are looking for.

Format of the enrichment response.

hasMore.description: Whether there are more results to paginate through

nextCursor.description: The cursor to paginate through the next set of results

count.description:
Number of Items the Search will attempt to find.

The actual number of Items found may be less than this number depending on the query complexity.

query.description:
Natural language search query describing what you are looking for.

Be specific and descriptive about your requirements, characteristics, and any constraints that help narrow down the results.

Any URLs provided will be crawled and used as additional context for the search.

criteria.description:
Criteria every item is evaluated against.

It's not required to provide your own criteria, we automatically detect the criteria from all the information provided in the query. Only use this when you need more fine control.

exclude.description: Sources (existing imports or websets) to exclude from search results. Any results found within these sources will be omitted to prevent finding them during search.

id.description: The ID of the source to exclude.

scope.description: Limit the search to specific sources (existing imports). Any results found within these sources matching the search criteria will be included in the Webset.

id.description: The ID of the source to search.

definition.description: What the relationship of the entities you hope to find is relative to the entities contained in the provided source.

recall.description:
Whether to provide an estimate of how many total relevant results could exist for this search.
Result of the analysis will be available in the `recall` field within the search request.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs you want to associate with this object.

cadence.description: How often the monitor will run

cron.description: Cron expression for monitor cadence (must be a valid Unix cron with 5 fields). The schedule must trigger at most once per day.

behavior.description: Behavior to perform when monitor runs

config.description:
Specify the search parameters for the Monitor.

By default, the search parameters (query, entity and criteria) from the last search are used when no parameters are provided.

query.description: The query to search for. By default, the query from the last search is used.

criteria.description: The criteria to search for. By default, the criteria from the last search is used.

count.description: The maximum number of results to find

behavior.description: The behaviour of the Search when it is added to a Webset.

hasMore.description: Whether there are more results to paginate through

nextCursor.description: The cursor to paginate through the next set of results

cron.description: Cron expression for monitor cadence (must be a valid Unix cron with 5 fields). The schedule must trigger at most once per day.

config.description:
Specify the search parameters for the Monitor.

By default, the search parameters (query, entity and criteria) from the last search are used when no parameters are provided.

query.description: The query to search for. By default, the query from the last search is used.

criteria.description: The criteria to search for. By default, the criteria from the last search is used.

count.description: The maximum number of results to find

behavior.description: The behaviour of the Search when it is added to a Webset.

hasMore.description: Whether there are more results to paginate through

nextCursor.description: The cursor to paginate through the next set of results

size.description: The size of the file in bytes. Maximum size is 50 MB.

count.description: The number of records to import

format.description: When the import is in CSV format, we expect a column containing the key identifier for the entity - for now URL. If not provided, import will fail to be processed.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs you want to associate with this object.

entity.description: What type of entity the import contains (e.g. People, Companies, etc.), and thus should be attempted to be resolved as.

csv.description: When format is `csv`, these are the specific import parameters.

identifier.description: Column containing the key identifier for the entity (e.g. URL, Name, etc.). If not provided, we will try to infer it from the file.

CreateImportResponse.description: The response to a successful import. Includes the upload URL and the upload valid until date.

id.description: The unique identifier for the Import

entity.description: The type of entity the import contains.

count.description: The number of entities in the import

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs you want to associate with this object.

failedMessage.description: A human readable message of the import failure

createdAt.description: When the import was created

updatedAt.description: When the import was last updated

uploadUrl.description: The URL to upload the file to

uploadValidUntil.description: The date and time until the upload URL is valid. The upload URL will be valid for 1 hour.

hasMore.description: Whether there are more results to paginate through

nextCursor.description: The cursor to paginate through the next set of results
